column4: NASA-CR-4538 Section 3.1.3 Hysteresis Loss, Laminations; Tempel Steel Company manufacturer data selected for the report's laminated compressor return-path iron
column5: The report identifies Tempel Steel Company product 24N208 as 0.025 in thick non-oriented annealed nominal 3 percent silicon steel of AISI M19 grade and prints a 2.08 W/lbm rating at 15 kG, 60 Hz, and 50 percent w/ the grain. Exact SI normalization uses 1 lbm=0.45359237 kg, 1 G=1e-4 T, and 1 in=0.0254 m. The phrase 50 percent w/ the grain is preserved as source context because the report does not define its sampling or orientation semantics. The report attributes the input to manufacturer data and does not identify the manufacturer sheet or revision, product lot, heat chemistry, anneal schedule, surface insulation or coating, magnetic test method, waveform, test temperature, repeats, dispersion, uncertainty, or whether the printed rating is a maximum, typical, mean, or single result. It also does not say whether the rating is hysteresis-only or total core loss. The report authors' later 8 W core and 10 W shell assembly estimates are model outputs and are not admitted as material claims. This state is not fused with NASA-TN-D-8184's anonymous M19 material deck
